project.title: Forge Space MCP Gateway Commons Infrastructure
project.summary: Forge Space is building a self-hosted Model Context Protocol gateway and governance layer that helps small teams aggregate MCP servers, route tools safely, and keep agent workflows interoperable.
project.problem: Teams adopting agentic tooling hit IDE tool limits, fragmented authentication, weak governance, and a fast-growing surface area of MCP servers that are hard to combine safely.
project.solution: The funded work focuses on the open mcp-gateway stack: self-hosted aggregation, registry-aware onboarding, policy controls, auditable routing, and maintainable deployment patterns that reduce lock-in.
project.fit: The work is MIT-licensed, self-hostable, public-by-default, and improves interoperability for the broader MCP ecosystem rather than a closed hosted-only product.
project.audience: Open-source maintainers, small product teams, internal platform teams, and consultants adopting MCP-based workflows.

repos.name: mcp-gateway
repos.role: Self-hosted MCP gateway, routing, registration, and admin operations

repos.name: core
repos.role: Shared governance patterns, scorecards, and release conventions

repos.name: ui-mcp
repos.role: Reference MCP server used for package and registry distribution validation

deliverables: A reproducible self-hosted gateway deployment path with documented hardening and registry-aware server onboarding.
Open governance and routing patterns for MCP aggregation, including auditable policy and scorecard hooks.
Reference packaging and discovery flows for Forge Space MCP servers so public distribution stays aligned with self-hosted infrastructure.
Public documentation, example stacks, and dissemination updates that help external teams adopt the gateway.

milestones.name: Gateway hardening and deployment baseline
outcomes: Self-host deployment baseline with reproducible install path
Operational validation for auth, health, and failure recovery

milestones.name: Registry-aware interoperability flow
outcomes: Registry-aware onboarding and validation flow for MCP servers
Reference package and metadata automation for public MCP servers
Cross-repo compatibility checks across gateway and server packages

milestones.name: Governance, routing, and audit surfaces
outcomes: Policy and scorecard guidance for self-hosted agent workflows
Public examples for safe MCP aggregation

milestones.name: Documentation and dissemination
outcomes: Public docs, demos, and adoption guides
Design-partner feedback capture and iteration log
Final public project update with adoption guidance

budget.label: Core engineering and maintenance
budget.rationale: Implementation, packaging, validation, and release work across the gateway and supporting repos.

budget.label: Documentation and dissemination
budget.rationale: Public docs, examples, operator guides, and external communication.

budget.label: Design-partner validation
budget.rationale: Structured feedback, testing time, and adoption-oriented iteration with early users.

budget.label: Infrastructure and operational costs
budget.rationale: Release infrastructure, package distribution, observability, and lightweight hosting needed to validate the open stack.

sustainability.distribution: Distribution is open-source first: GitHub repos, npm packages, MCP Registry metadata, docs, and public discussions feed the acquisition loop.
sustainability.maintenance: Forge Space maintains the repositories in public and uses the same governance tooling internally, so funded outputs stay in the mainline repos instead of a one-off grant branch.
sustainability.postGrant: The longer-term commercial surface is optional hosted support and design-partner work, while the funded deliverables remain self-hostable and openly documented.
